zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Burgh於1488年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛時，他的父親Edward de Burgh Sir, 2nd Lord of Gainsborough, 11th ggf25歲，母親Anne Cobham20歲。他於大約1507年與Agnes Tyrwhitt在英國林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他於1518年被列為high sheriff of lincolnshire。他去世於1550年2月28日在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas Burgh于1488年出生在英国英格兰林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛时，他的父亲Edward de Burgh Sir, 2nd Lord of Gainsborough, 11th ggf25岁，母亲Anne Cobham20岁。他于大约1507年与Agnes Tyrwhitt在英国林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他于1518年被列为high sheriff of lincolnshire。他去世于1550年2月28日在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Burgh was born in 1488, in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Edward de Burgh Sir, 2nd Lord of Gainsborough, 11th ggf, was 25 and his mother, Anne Cobham, was 20. He married Agnes Tyrwhitt about 1507, in Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 6 daughters. In 1518, his occupation is listed as high sheriff of lincolnshire. He died on 28 February 1550, in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas Burgh est né en 1488, à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edward de Burgh Sir, 2nd Lord of Gainsborough, 11th ggf, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Anne Cobham, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Tyrwhitt environ 1507, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 6 filles. En 1518, sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of lincolnshire. Il est décédé le 28 février 1550, à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

Er hat um 1507 in Lincolnshire, England Agnes Tyrwhitt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 6 Töchter. 1518 ist sein Beruf als high sheriff of lincolnshire aufgeführt. Er starb am 28. Februar 1550 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Burgh è nato il nel 1488 nel Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Edward de Burgh Sir, 2nd Lord of Gainsborough, 11th ggf, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Anne Cobham, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Tyrwhitt circa 1507, nel Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 6 figlie. Nel 1518, la sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of lincolnshire. Morì il 28 febbraio 1550, nel Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ko.of Saltfleetby, Lincolnshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 살트플릿비 스트 클레멘트
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Burgh(이)가 1488년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 게인스버루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward de Burgh Sir, 2nd Lord of Gainsborough, 11th ggf(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Cobham(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1507년 영국 링코실르에서 Agnes Tyrwhitt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1518년, 그의 직업은 high sheriff of lincolnshire이었다. 그는 1550년 2월 28일 영국 링코실르 게인스버루에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Agnes Tyrwhitt aproximadamente 1507, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 6 filhas. Em 1518, sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of lincolnshire. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas Burgh родился в 1488 г. в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Edward de Burgh Sir, 2nd Lord of Gainsborough, 11th ggf было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Cobham, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Tyrwhitt около 1507 г. в местности линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 6 дочерей. В 1518 году он работал по профессии high sheriff of lincolnshire. Он умер 28 февраля 1550 г. в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.of Saltfleetby, Lincolnshire, England: Saltfleetby St Clement, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas Burgh nació en 1488, en Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Edward de Burgh Sir, 2nd Lord of Gainsborough, 11th ggf, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Anne Cobham, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Agnes Tyrwhitt aproximadamente 1507, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 6 hijas. En 1518, su ocupación registrada era high sheriff of lincolnshire. Murió el 28 de febrero de 1550, en Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
